free_text: Excitation Functions of Nuclear Reactions Induced by
Alpha Particles up to 42 MeV on natTi for Monitoring
Puposes and TLA
, CGR 560 Cyclotron in Brussels
, MGC 20E Cyclotron in Debrecen
High purity Ti foils, 12, 12.5 and 21.6 microns thick
provided by Goodfellow.
Stacks containing several Ti foils were irradiated
nn Faraday cup type target holder. Irradiation time
was 10-120 minutes. Beam current of 50-2000 nA was
applied.
The energy of the incident beam was determined by using
Time-Of-Flight method and activation of monitor foils.
Alltogether 7 stacks were irradiated.
Decay data were taken from Browne and Firestone,
Table of Radioactive Isotopes, V.S. Shirley, Wiley,
London, 1986.
The effective bombarding energy in each foil of the
stack was calculated using the polynomial approximation
of Ziegler et.al.
Energy uncertainyy was calculated taking into
account uncertainty of the initial energy, beam
straggling, foil thickness and uniformity.
The uncertainty on each cross section was
estimated by taking the square root of the summation
of the contributing sources in quadrature. (10-16%)
Number of target atoms           5%
Nuclear data                     5%
Beam intensity                   7%
Detector efficiency            3-7%
Statistics and peak fitting   1-10%
Data were provided by the authors
Approved by S. Takacs 17-April-2001.
Ref. corrected.
